metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_1001
metadata.comment:
Rationale: The paper makes use of a conditionally-floxed beta-catenin CKO mouse line. The conditional deletion is achieved via a CaMKII-cre transgenic allele (this results in a deletion at around P18).
Figure 2: electron microscopy of stratum radiatum synapses shows:
- a 25% increase in the number of excitatory, spine synapses (2A,B,D) in the CKO.
- a 3-fold increase in perforated synapses (2C,E) in the CKO.
- a 40% reduction in the undocked pool of vesicles per synapse (2F) in the CKO.
- The number of docked vesicles located less than one vesicle diameter from
the presynaptic membrane was similar (Figures 2A, 2B, and 2F).
- The reduction in the number of vesicles per synapse in the CKO is not due to redistribution of vesicles over a larger number of synapses. Quantification of the total number of vesicles (in a defined region) shows a 30% reduction in the total number of vesicles.

Taken together these data suggest a presynaptic role of beta-catenin in vesicle clustering at the reserve pool region adjacent to the active zone.. Experimental description: 'To generate mice lacking β-catenin in excitatory neurons in the hippocampus, mice with two loxP-flanked β-catenin alleles (β-catenin flox/flox) were crossed with mice in which CamKII regulatory elements control cre recombinase expression (CamKcre).'
